sections.content:
Looking at the image, the dark gray shape has moved to the right and is now overlapping with the player shape! The dark gray is now at the divider/player boundary. The dark gray L-shape is crossing the divider.
Let me check the exact positions to see if the dark gray fully overlaps with the player.
The animation_changed_bbox is [18, 27, 56, 63], covering rows 18-56 and cols 27-63. This includes the dark gray and player areas.
Let me check the segmentation to see the current positions.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current positions after 5 LEFTs
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("Current shapes:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] > 5:
        print(f"  color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! The dark gray (c) is now split into TWO separate pieces:
1. c, 18 pixels: boundary=[[18, 39], [18, 44], [20, 44], [20, 39]] - a 3x5 block at rows 18-20, cols 39-44
2. c, 18 pixels: boundary=[[27, 30], [27, 35], [29, 35], [29, 30]] - a 3x5 block at rows 27-29, cols 30-35
The player (B, 64 pixels) is at boundary=[[18, 30], [18, 38], [26, 38], [27, 39], [27, 44], [29, 44], [29, 36], [21, 36], [20, 35], [20, 30]] - this is the player shape at rows 18-29, cols 30-44.
The dark gray pieces are now inside/overlapping with the player shape! The total dark gray pixels went from 72 to 18+18=36. So half the dark gray pixels are gone (they're now inside the player and merged/hidden).
Wait, actually the dark gray L-shape has 72 pixels. Now it's split into two 18-pixel pieces, totaling 36 pixels. The other 36 pixels must be hidden/merged with the player.
The divider (S) is now split into two parts:
1. S, 48 pixels: at cols 36-38, rows 0-17 (top part)
2. S, 88 pixels: at cols 36-38, rows 30-62 (bottom part)
The middle part of the divider (rows 18-29) is gone, probably because the dark gray/player overlap covers it.
So the dark gray is now overlapping with the player. This seems like progress! But the level is not completed yet. Maybe I need to press SPACE or ACTION7 to "confirm" the overlap?
Actually, let me reconsider. In level 1, I moved the dark gray to overlap with the yellow, and the level completed. Here, I moved the dark gray to overlap with the player, but the level didn't complete. Maybe the goal is different.
Wait, looking at the image, the dark gray is now on the RIGHT side of the divider, overlapping with the player. The yellow is still on the left side. Maybe I need to move the dark gray to overlap with the YELLOW, not the player.
But the dark gray and yellow are on opposite sides of the divider, and the divider seems to be a barrier. Maybe I need to move the dark gray back to the left side to overlap with the yellow?
Let me try moving the dark gray back to the left using RIGHT (which moves it away from the player, i.e., leftward).
Actually wait - the dark gray is now split into two pieces. Maybe I need to move it further right to fully overlap with the player? Or maybe I need to move it to a specific position.
Let me try pressing SPACE or ACTION7 to see if the overlap triggers something.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:

<function=python>
<parameter=code>

# Level not completed. Try SPACE to confirm?
action(['SPACE'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
